web.markdown:
[❤️Show some love](https://www.buymeacoffee.com/sameerasw)

[Back to top](https://sameerasw.com/zen#)

# Zen Zero

## Zen Browser

The most minimal a browser can be. Zen Zero is a customized **Zen browser** which aim to keep away the distractions and focus on the web and make it look pretty with transparency.

Zen Zero - Zen Browser Transparency Setup - YouTube

Tap to unmute

[Zen Zero - Zen Browser Transparency Setup](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4Pjk_EsT9TE) [Sameera Wijerathna](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCJ_esHlbZgQ-iQHZGWE-fCQ)

![thumbnail-image](https://yt3.ggpht.com/bCHa228-kWzX82i9go-Bqh5IppDsy-P22Vglai1WqWuh8fAGlQtfACD1wkbua7yWvlS0Z2VB=s68-c-k-c0x00ffffff-no-rj)

Sameera Wijerathna219 subscribers

![Zen Browser Preview 1](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/preview-1.jpeg)

![Zen Browser Preview 2](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/preview-2.jpeg)

❮❯

This is a guide to install the **Zen Internet Addon** and make your Zen browser transparent. This is a work in progress and I will be adding more features and fixes as I go. If you have any issues, please reach out to me on any of my social media or community groups.

1. ## Install the Transparent Zen mod


Install the **[Transparent Zen mod _open\_in\_new_](https://zen-browser.app/mods/642854b5-88b4-4c40-b256-e035532109df/)** from the mod store.
    [![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/zen-mod.jpeg)](https://zen-browser.app/mods/642854b5-88b4-4c40-b256-e035532109df/)
2. ## Go to mod settings


Once you have installed the mod, Go into Zen Browser settings> Mods > Transparent Zen and click on the **Settings** button.
![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/zen-settings.jpeg)
3. ## Configure the mod settings


Then make sure the **Allow zen browser to be transparent** option is checked. (And if you are on Linux, you may see another toggle to be turned on as well). Enable other options if you like and then quit and **re-open the zen browser** to apply the changes.
![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/mod-settings-config.jpeg)
4. ## Install Zen Internet Addon


Go to the firefox addons page and install the **[Zen Internet Addon _open\_in\_new_](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/zen-internet/)**.
    [![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/zen-internet-addon.jpeg)](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/zen-internet/)
5. ## Sync and Fetch the latest themes


Open the addon pop-up and click on the **Refetch latest styles** button to fetch the initial styles and keep the auto update toggle on to keep them up to date as I make changes.
![](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/zen-internet-sync.jpeg)

Congratulations

Your browser is now transparent

At least I hope so

You can now enjoy the zen browser with a transparent background. You can also adjust various features provided for each website when you open the pop-up in that webpage.

## Troubleshooting

- At the moment, only some of the operating systems are known to support transparency. Here are some:

  - macOS
  - Windows 11 - native mica and acrylic
  - Windows 11 - With mods like **[mica for everyone _open\_in\_new_](https://github.com/MicaForEveryone/MicaForEveryone)** or **[DWM blur glass _open\_in\_new_](https://github.com/Maplespe/DWMBlurGlass)**
  - Windows 10 - With **[DWM blur glass _open\_in\_new_](https://github.com/Maplespe/DWMBlurGlass)**
  - Wayland on **[KDE with Kvin blur _open\_in\_new_](https://www.reddit.com/r/zen_browser/comments/1jd0ggm/comment/miemhvg/?utm_source=share&utm_medium=web3x&utm_name=web3xcss&utm_term=1&utm_content=share_button)**
  - Hyprland - instructions



    **Tested on version 1.14.8b flatpak version**



    **Step 1: Enable flags in about:config**



    - widget.transparent-windows
    - zen.theme.gradient.show-custom-colors
    - zen.theme.acrylic-elements (not sure)

**Step 2: Install Transparent Zen mod**(enable transparency features for linux as well)

**Step 3: Use Nebula configs**(Nebula.uc.js userChrome.css userContent.css Nebula/)

**Step 4: Configure Hyprland transparency**\- Set opacity to 0.9 (exact value doesn't matter much)
- If you are using a different OS, please let me know if it works or not.

- Check your power saving settings and make sure that’s off on Windows.
![Windows power saving settings](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/win-power-saving.jpg)
- Make sure your Windows color settings are not affecting transparency.
![Windows color settings](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/win-color.jpeg)
- Hard to read text? black text in dark mode? It's because that website is not in dark theme or doesn't have one at all. In that case, you can use the **[Darkreader addon _open\_in\_new_](https://darkreader.org/)** to fix this behavior.
![Darkreader](https://repository-images.githubusercontent.com/26682105/d6f7dbef-46e9-4fa0-8961-8b73d1b53167)

## Site specific features

Once you start browsing, Check the add-on pop-up on each website and you will see a list of features along with transparency.

![Zen Internet Addon](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/site-specific.jpeg)

## Theme request and issues

- Don’t see transparency for your favorite website? Request a theme.

- You can report bugs and theme issues from the addon as well.
![Zen Internet Addon](https://sameerasw.com/assets/img/articles/zen/notion/request-2.jpeg)
- Restricted websites and pages can not be themed and your requests will be closed.


## More Resources

[article\\
Legacy guide](https://sameerasw.notion.site/Zen-Transparency-1939c6099d4080468f02cf05ae50e827) [mail\\
Contact me](https://sameerasw.com/#contact) [news\\
Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/zen_browser)

web.title: Blur Shenanigans - ZeStig's lair
web.description: Here's what the Transparent Zen settings menu looks like (as of time of this post). aboutconfig.jpg. Restart Zen after making these changes.
web.markdown:
* * *

# Blur Shenanigans

6 October 2025


323 words

~2 mins

[#NixOS](https://zstg.is-a.dev/tags/nixos) [#zen-browser](https://zstg.is-a.dev/tags/zen-browser)

**Table of Contents**

## My triumphant return to Hyprland

First off, I'm really happy to announce that I've been using Hyprland for around a week now. I'd taken a hiatus for a few months. I'd been using KDE Plasma during that time. Not sure what got into me, but I've come back to Hyprland. I'm also giving the COSMIC DE a try. I'm on NixOS Unstable, and so I'm daily-driving [1.0.0-beta1](https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/blob/d7434fa95a4f55e5f6cf6185134cd9d00130ef2c/pkgs/by-name/co/cosmic-comp/package.nix) as of time of writing this.

## Setting up blur on Zen

I wholly acknowledge [SlipStream](https://mastodon.social/@slipstream8125)'s help setting this up. With that out of the way, let's start!

### Get the addon and mod

Grab the Zen internet Firefox addon from [here](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/zen-internet/).

In addition to this, you'll also require the [Transparent Zen mod](https://zen-browser.app/mods/642854b5-88b4-4c40-b256-e035532109df):

### Configuration

Ensure that these flags are set in `about:config`:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Configuration | Value |
| `browser.tabs.allow_transparent_browser` | true |
| `mod.sameerasw.zen_transparent_glance_enabled` | true |
| `mod.sameerasw.zen_transparent_sidebar_enabled` | true |
| `widget.transparent_windows` | true |
| `zen.widget.linux.transparency` | true |

Here's what the `Transparent Zen` settings menu looks like (as of time of this post).

![aboutconfig.jpg](https://zstg.is-a.dev/posts/linux/nixos/blur-shenanigans/aboutconfig.jpg)

Restart Zen after making these changes.

### Configuring your DE/WM

#### KDE Plasma

Firstly, install the [BetterBlur Plasma effect](https://github.com/taj-ny/kwin-effects-forceblur). Since I'm [on NixOS](https://zstg.is-a.dev/posts/linux/nixos/nixos-part-2/), I've added [this](https://git.sr.ht/~zstg/dots/tree/91a17cd758c89692f4cae833b7ad534e35b3df07/item/hosts/Krypton/services/desktopmanager/kde-plasma/default.nix#L7) to [my config](https://git.sr.ht/~zstg/dots/tree/91a17cd758c89692f4cae833b7ad534e35b3df07/item/flake.nix#L42-45).

Then I've configured blur like so. Note that **I've disabled the builtin blur effect as it conflicts with the effect we've just installed**.

![Plasma1.jpg](https://zstg.is-a.dev/posts/linux/nixos/blur-shenanigans/Plasma1.jpg)![Plasma2.jpg](https://zstg.is-a.dev/posts/linux/nixos/blur-shenanigans/Plasma2.jpg)

#### Hyprland

Thankfully, it's rather easy to get this working on Hyprland.

~~Configure a window rule and change the opacity of all zen windows:~~

> **EDIT**: _It appears you_ **do not need** _to add the window rule I've shown below._

```nix
windowrulev2 = opacity 0.95, class:^(${MOZ_APP_LAUNCHER:-zen-beta})$, initialtitle: $(Zen Browser)$ # blur?
```

… and take a look at my blur settings:

```nix
blur {
      enabled = true
      size = 6
      passes = 3
      new_optimizations = true
    }
    blurls = zen-beta
    blurls = ghostty
    blurls = vicinae
    shadow {
      enabled = true
      range = 4
      render_power = 3
      color = rgba(1a1a1aee)
   }
```

### … and the finished setup looks like

![zenblur.png](https://zstg.is-a.dev/posts/linux/nixos/blur-shenanigans/zenblur.png)

and here's my own blog (because why not 😉) - [link to the post shown here](http://zstg.is-a.dev/posts/emacs/notmuch/).

![blog.jpg](https://zstg.is-a.dev/posts/linux/nixos/blur-shenanigans/blog.jpg)

Back to Top

[← prev](https://zstg.is-a.dev/posts/emacs/notmuch/ "Setting up Gmail with Notmuch")

web.title: Transparent Zen – Get this Extension for Firefox (en-US)
web.description: Download Transparent Zen for Firefox. Applies custom styles to make your favorite websites transparent.
web.markdown:
![New Tab (Bonjour add-on)](https://addons.mozilla.org/user-media/previews/thumbs/318/318880.jpg?modified=1744799872)![reddit.com](https://addons.mozilla.org/user-media/previews/thumbs/318/318881.jpg?modified=1744799859)![github.com](https://addons.mozilla.org/user-media/previews/thumbs/318/318882.jpg?modified=1744799861)![Extension popup with settings](https://addons.mozilla.org/user-media/previews/thumbs/318/318883.jpg?modified=1744799823)

The idea behind this project is to make it as simple as possible for everyone to have make their websites transparent and to better match the rest of the browser better.

Prerequisites:

Windows 11

1\. Open Zen Browser and go to about:config

2\. Make sure that browser.tabs.allow\_transparent\_browser, widget.transparent-windows and widget.windows.mica are all set to true

3\. Install MicaForEveryone (optional)

3.1. In MicaForEveryone add a new process rule and select "zen"

3.2. Activate Blur Behind and set the Backdrop Type to Acrylic

Linux

1\. Open Zen Browser and go to about:config. Ensure the installed version of Zen is above release 1.11.2b

2\. Make sure that browser.tabs.allow\_transparent\_browser and zen.widget.linux.transparency are set to true

3\. For a blurred window with KDE PLASMA, install kwin-effects-forceblur through your system repository, or build from source

3.1. Follow steps shown on the project Github to enable blur, and add zen to the allowlist

With version 0.2.0 an early approach for dynamic transparency was added which aims to dynamically make any website transparent. Please keep in mind that this feature isn't finished yet and is still being worked on.

- [ddl-warez.cc](https://prod.outgoing.prod.webservices.mozgcp.net/v1/cdec4eca74b6145680dda8a7eb6dcdb27f42a9d877fdb4cd498656a026cb5916/http%3A//ddl-warez.cc)
